title: Geographic Information Systems Technologists and Technicians
description: Assist scientists or related professionals in building, maintaining, modifying, or using geographic information systems (GIS) databases. May also perform some custom application development or provide user support.

tasks.text: Produce data layers, maps, tables, or reports, using spatial analysis procedures or Geographic Information Systems (GIS) technology, equipment, or systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Design or prepare graphic representations of Geographic Information Systems (GIS) data, using GIS hardware or software applications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Maintain or modify existing Geographic Information Systems (GIS) databases.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide technical expertise in Geographic Information Systems (GIS) technology to clients or users.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform computer programming, data analysis, or software development for Geographic Information Systems (GIS) applications, including the maintenance of existing systems or research and development for future enhancements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Enter data into Geographic Information Systems (GIS) databases, using techniques such as coordinate geometry, keyboard entry of tabular data, manual digitizing of maps, scanning or automatic conversion to vectors, or conversion of other sources of digital data.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review existing or incoming data for currency, accuracy, usefulness, quality, or completeness of documentation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Perform geospatial data building, modeling, or analysis, using advanced spatial analysis, data manipulation, or cartography software.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Design or coordinate the development of integrated Geographic Information Systems (GIS) spatial or non-spatial databases.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform integrated or computerized Geographic Information Systems (GIS) analyses to address scientific problems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select cartographic elements needed for effective presentation of information.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide technical support to users or clients regarding the maintenance, development, or operation of Geographic Information Systems (GIS) databases, equipment, or applications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collect, compile, or integrate Geographic Information Systems (GIS) data, such as remote sensing or cartographic data for inclusion in map manuscripts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Interpret aerial or ortho photographs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Meet with clients to discuss topics such as technical specifications, customized solutions, or operational problems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Document, design, code, or test Geographic Information Systems (GIS) models, internet mapping solutions, or other applications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Create, analyze, report, convert, or transfer data, using specialized applications program software.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Confer with users to analyze, configure, or troubleshoot applications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Design, program, or model Geographic Information Systems (GIS) applications or procedures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop specialized computer software routines, internet-based Geographic Information Systems (GIS) databases, or business applications to customize geographic information.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Make recommendations regarding upgrades, considering implications of new or revised Geographic Information Systems (GIS) software, equipment, or applications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assist users in formulating Geographic Information Systems (GIS) requirements or understanding the implications of alternatives.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Create visual representations of geospatial data, using complex procedures such as analytical modeling, three-dimensional renderings, or plot creation.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Transfer or rescale information from original photographs onto maps or other photographs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare training materials for, or make presentations to, Geographic Information Systems (GIS) users.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Apply three-dimensional (3D) or four-dimensional (4D) technologies to geospatial data to allow for new or different analyses or applications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct research, data analysis, systems design, or support for software such as Geographic Information Systems (GIS) or Global Positioning Systems (GPS) mapping software.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Read current literature, talk with colleagues, continue education, or participate in professional organizations or conferences to keep abreast of developments in Geographic Information Systems (GIS) technology, equipment, or systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Recommend procedures, equipment, or software upgrades to increase data accessibility or ease of use.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
